sections.paragraph:
fut. יַעֲשֹׁק, Arab. عسق.-

(1) to oppress, to act towards, or treat any one unjustly or violently, e.g. the needy, helpless, Pro 14:31 22:16 28:3 Ecc 4:1 a king his subjects, 1Sa 12:3, 1Sa 12:4a victor, the vanquished, Isa 52:4 Jer 50:33 Psa 105:14 Hos 5:11 God, a man, Job 10:3. Metaph. Pro 28:17, אָדָס עָשׁוּק בְּדַס־נֶפֶשׁ “a man oppressed with life blood” (which he has shed), i.e. bowed down under this guilt as a burden.

(2) to defraud, any one, to extort from him by fraud and violence, with an acc. of pers. Lev 19:13 Deu 24:14 and of the thing, Mal 3:5, עשְׁקֵי שְׂכַר שָׂכִיר “who extort the wages of the hireling.”-Both constructions (Nos. 1 and 2) are found together in Mic 2:2, עָֽשְׁקוּ גֶּבֶר וּבֵיתֹו “they oppress a man and wrest away his house,” i.e. act both with fraud and violence, compare גָּזַל.

(3) to be proud, insolent, metaph. of a river overflowing its banks, Job 40:23 (compare syn. بغا, ظلم, فجر).

Pual, part. מְעֻשָּׁקָה (virgin) violated forcibly, metaph. of a captured city, Isa 23:12.

Derivatives, מַעֲשַׁקֹּות, עָשֹׁוק, עֲשׁוּקִיס, עשֶׁק, עָשְׁקָה pr.n. עֵשֶׁק.
